locations.name: 中非共和國
locations.summary: 中非共和國（法語：République centrafricaine；桑戈語：Ködörösêse tî Bêafrîka），通稱中非，是位於非洲中部的國家。作為一內陸國家，中非從西邊起依順時針方向，分別与喀麥隆、乍得、蘇丹、南蘇丹、剛果…

locations.name: 撒哈拉沙漠
locations.summary: 撒哈拉沙漠（阿拉伯語：الصحراء الكبرى，aṣ-Ṣaḥrāʾ al-Kubrā ， 清朝直譯大沙漠）位於非洲大陸北部，是世界最热的荒漠，亦是世界第三大荒漠，仅次于南极和北极，同时也是世界上最大的沙漠，其总面积超过9,400,00…

locations.name: 奈及利亞
locations.summary: 奈及利亞聯邦共和國（英語：Federal Republic of Nigeria），通称奈及利亞（英語：Nigeria），是西非國家，位於非洲的幾內亞灣西岸頂點，鄰國包括西邊的贝宁，北邊的尼日尔，東北方與乍得接壤一小段國界，正東則是喀麥隆…

locations.name: 马里
locations.summary: 馬里，全名马里共和国（法語：République du Mali），是位於西非萨赫勒地区的内陆国家，北部地區北接阿尔及利亚、东接尼日尔、西面毛里塔尼亚，南部西接塞内加尔、西南接几内亚、南鄰布基纳法索和科特迪瓦，是西非面积第二大的国家。其北…

relations.subject: Verreaux's eagle-owl
relations.evidence: 2005"/> Eastward from those countries to the Central African Republic, the species is distributed in a narrow transitional zone between the Sahara and rainforests. Seemingly isolated populations occur in central Nigeria and central Mali.Buxton, P

relations.subject: Verreaux's eagle-owl
relations.evidence: Bird observation in the Ogaden Region Somali Regional State,/Ethiopia A Contribution to the Identification of Important Bird Areas. This species inhabits mainly savanna with scattered trees and thorny vegetation
relations.object_summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

relations.subject: Verreaux's eagle-owl
relations.evidence: Ostrich, 40(S1), 179-192. They also range into riverine forest adjacent to savanna and small, semi-open woodland surrounded by open country, though they are less likely to inhabit heavily wooded habitats. South African eagle-owls are not infrequently found around floodplains and marshes, which may provide the primary nesting habitat in some areas. In Uganda, they are largely associated with riparian woodlands.Seavy, N

relations.object_summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

relations.subject: Verreaux's eagle-owl
relations.evidence: Comparative study of general public owl knowledge in Costa Rica, Central America and Malawi, Africa. An additional threat is the residual effects of pesticides, as poison (usually through rodenticide or poisoned carcasses left out for scavengers such as jackals) consumed through prey may badly affect them.Herholdt, J
